paper_title: Concealed Canyons Delineation and Depositional Elements Analysis Applying a Multidisciplinary Geological and Geophysical Approach: A Case Study of the Pliocene Turbidites, West El-Burullus Area, Offshore Nile Delta, Egypt

methods.description: This method involves using seismic attributes such as spectral decomposition, RMS amplitude, and coherency to analyze the geomorphology and sedimentary facies distribution of the WEB canyon. It helps in understanding the paleo-depositional environment and detecting channels and their gas content.

steps.description: The coherency (variance) attribute measures the similarity in every point in a seismic cube to its contiguous points, by computing positional waveform similarity in both crossline and inline, which is used here to detect the discontinuity and muddy facies.
steps.searchable_summary: Compute coherency attribute to detect discontinuities and muddy facies in seismic data

steps.description: The classical attribute (RMS) is used to detect the bright spot, which is an indication either the lithology change or the lithology with the gas effect.
steps.searchable_summary: Calculate RMS amplitude to detect bright spots indicating lithology changes or gas effects

steps.description: Spectral decomposition is used for temporal mapping of bed thickness, determining stratigraphic traps, and delineation of hydrocarbon distribution.
steps.searchable_summary: Perform spectral decomposition for bed thickness mapping and hydrocarbon distribution

steps.description: In this study, the spectral decomposition with frequency blending in RGB colors is used for time slices at different successive levels between the canyon top and base to track the vertical and horizontal changes of the reservoir.
steps.searchable_summary: Visualize frequency cubes using RGB blending for reservoir analysis

methods.description: This method involves interpreting sedimentological facies and constructing a geological-depositional model of the WEB canyon based on well log data and seismic analysis.

steps.description: In the study area, there are distinct sedimentological facies interpreted at WEB-1 well showing multi-GR responses.
steps.searchable_summary: Interpret sedimentological facies from well log data

steps.description: The WEB canyon segmentation can represent from bottom to top: basal segment lobe, incision channel, amalgamated channels, and plugged muddy channels.
steps.searchable_summary: Model canyon segmentation based on sedimentological facies
